fields.description:
RED baseline test suite: inject the full LLM-failure matrix through the pydantic-ai `model_override` seam and pin rebar's CURRENT behavior, so later stories flip it to the new behavior with a regression net.

## Why
Before changing behavior we must pin what rebar does today for every failure class and prove the offline injection harness works. The brainstorm validated on pydantic-ai 1.107.0 that a `FunctionModel` which raises `ModelHTTPError(429)` propagates with `status_code` intact, a canned `finish_reason` is readable at `result.response.finish_reason`, and `httpx.ReadTimeout` propagates unwrapped — so the harness is viable with no live/billable call.

## What
A parametrized RED test file injecting each failure class via a raising or canned pydantic-ai `FunctionModel` through the `PydanticAIRunner(model_override=...)` / `get_runner(override=...)` seams (`src/rebar/llm/runner.py`), asserting current behavior at two levels: (1) runner classification (each failure surfaces as whatever typed error the runner raises TODAY — provider failures as `LLMUnavailableError`, the step-budget path as `LLMRunnerError`, structured-output failures as `StructuredOutputError`; the test pins the ACTUAL current type per class) and (2) gate end-to-end (plan-review systemic → INDETERMINATE + claim blocked; completion → fail-closed; code-review). Guarded by `pydantic_ai.models.ALLOW_MODEL_REQUESTS = False`.

## Scope
- Failure matrix: 429-rate, 429-insufficient_quota (body), 529, 500/503, connect-timeout, read-timeout, 401/403, 400 bad-request, 400 context-length, 413, content-filter finish_reason, a structured content-filter refusal, length/truncation, an incomplete-tool-call, unparseable output, validation-retry exhaustion, step-budget overrun, tool-call-limit overrun, unknown exception. (The structured content-filter and incomplete-tool-call cases raise the corresponding pydantic-ai exception types, imported from `pydantic_ai.exceptions` at test runtime — library exceptions exercised by import, not rebar symbols.)
- Two-scope scenarios: systemic (whole op) vs per-criterion (one unit among N; the plan-review pass-1 fail-open, where one flaky finder marks its criterion INDETERMINATE and the review continues).
- Named liveness scenarios pin CURRENT (pre-change) behavior only: a hung model request and a hung tool document that TODAY there is no per-request/per-tool timeout (the RED test asserts the current unbounded/hanging behavior, e.g. via a short outer test-level guard), and a looping model reaches the runner's DERIVED request budget. The NEW abort+classify assertions (the `run_sync`-compatible zombie-run defense) are added by story chief-contained-hoopoe's tests, NOT here — this baseline story must stay green on today's code.
- The step-budget/tool-call-limit cases set `cfg.max_iterations` explicitly and assert against the DERIVED limits the runner computes — `request_limit = max(1, ceil(cfg.max_iterations/2))` and `tool_calls_limit = max(8, cfg.max_iterations)` (runner.py) — NOT pydantic-ai's default 50.
- Guard tests: `--force` / `--force-close` / feature-disabled never enters the handling path.
- Coverage boundary: `model_override` injects at the pydantic-ai MODEL layer, which is ABOVE the httpx transport where the retry lives — so transport-layer retry is NOT exercisable here; it is covered by the transport-injection spike in story morbid-uncultured-arcticduck. This suite covers classification + gate behavior + silent-success, not the transport.
- NOT in scope: the NEW behavior (asserted GREEN by later stories) — this story pins CURRENT behavior only.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A parametrized test file (e.g. `tests/unit/test_llm_failure_matrix.py`) exercises every failure class via `model_override`, offline, guarded by `ALLOW_MODEL_REQUESTS=False`.
- [ ] Each case pins the current runner-level and gate-level behavior with an inline `# CURRENT(<file>:<line>): <one-line behavior>` comment citing the exact source seam it pins; a meta-test asserts a `# CURRENT(` marker exists for each parametrized id (that inline comment IS the "source citation" — documentation of the pinned seam, verified by the meta-test, not a runtime assertion).
- [ ] The step-budget / tool-call-limit cases set `cfg.max_iterations` explicitly and assert against the runner's DERIVED `request_limit`/`tool_calls_limit`, never a hardcoded 50.
- [ ] Two-scope (systemic vs per-criterion) and force/disabled guard scenarios are covered.
- [ ] `make test` passes; the file is the named baseline that later stories flip to the new behavior.
